task_completion.description: Firefox is open and accessing the local Jitsi Meet instance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Firefox browser is visible with 'http://localhost:8080' in the address bar, or the Jitsi Meet homepage is displayed.

task_completion.description: The correct room name is entered to create the meeting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'TeamStandup' is typed into the 'Enter room name' input field on the Jitsi homepage, OR 'TeamStandup' is appended directly to the URL in the Firefox address bar.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates the process of joining the newly created room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Start meeting' / 'Join' button is clicked, or the browser shows a pre-join screen (e.g., asking for camera/microphone permissions or a display name).

task_completion.description: The agent has successfully entered the meeting room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Jitsi meeting interface is fully loaded. Look for the central video/avatar area and the bottom toolbar containing call controls (microphone, camera, and a red hang-up button).

task_completion.description: The active meeting room is confirmed to be 'TeamStandup'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The room name 'TeamStandup' is visible at the top of the Jitsi meeting interface (in the header/subject area), OR the Firefox address bar explicitly shows the URL ending in '/TeamStandup'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Firefox graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Firefox browser window. The agent should not bypass the UI by exclusively using terminal commands (like curl) to interact with the server.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Jitsi Meet web application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown must be the actual Jitsi Meet UI, not a generic text editor, a locally mocked HTML file, or a different video conferencing tool.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the room name.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Firefox Developer Tools (Inspect Element) panel should not be open and used to artificially inject the text 'TeamStandup' into the DOM to fake task completion.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires the agent to create and join a Jitsi meeting room named 'TeamStandup' on a local instance running at 'http://localhost:8080'. Successful completion must show the agent inside the active Jitsi meeting interface with the room name 'TeamStandup' visible either in the Jitsi header or as the final path in the Firefox address bar (e.g., http://localhost:8080/TeamStandup).
